document:
Incident Type: FIRE
Primary Department: Fire Department
Secondary Departments: Facility Management, Security Team
Severity: HIGH
Response Actions: Trigger fire alarm immediately. Notify the Fire Department immediately. Notify Facility Management so building systems can be checked or shut down if needed. Notify Security Team to assist with evacuation and perimeter control. Begin evacuation protocol if people are present.

Incident Type: THEFT_OR_ROBBERY
Primary Department: Security Team
Secondary Departments: Local Police Department
Severity: MEDIUM to HIGH
Response Actions: Notify Security Team immediately. If weapon presence, direct threats, or active robbery are observed, notify Local Police Department immediately. Preserve video evidence and continue tracking suspect movement through available cameras. Avoid direct confrontation unless trained staff are already engaged under protocol.

Incident Type: VIOLENCE_OR_ASSAULT
Primary Department: Security Team
Secondary Departments: Local Police Department, Medical Response Team
Severity: HIGH
Response Actions: Notify Security Team immediately. Notify Local Police Department if the conflict is active or dangerous. Notify Medical Response Team if any victim appears injured, unconscious, or unable to leave safely. Preserve footage and track participants through cameras where possible.

Incident Type: MEDICAL_EMERGENCY
Primary Department: Medical Response Team
Secondary Departments: Emergency Services, Security Team
Severity: HIGH
Response Actions: Notify Medical Response Team immediately. Notify Emergency Services if the person is unresponsive or the situation appears severe. Notify Security Team to clear the area and assist responders. Continue monitoring the scene for updates that may help responders.

Incident Type: SUSPICIOUS_BEHAVIOR
Primary Department: Security Monitoring Team
Secondary Departments: Security Team
Severity: LOW to MEDIUM
Response Actions: Notify Security Monitoring Team for live review. Escalate to Security Team if the behavior intensifies or involves restricted zones. Continue observation and preserve footage. Do not escalate to emergency services unless the situation develops into a direct threat.

Incident Type: UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS
Primary Department: Security Team
Secondary Departments: Facility Management
Severity: MEDIUM
Response Actions: Notify Security Team immediately. Notify Facility Management if locks, doors, or access systems may need to be checked. Track the individual through cameras and preserve footage. Secure the access point if safe to do so.

Incident Type: PROPERTY_DAMAGE
Primary Department: Facility Management
Secondary Departments: Security Team
Severity: LOW to MEDIUM
Response Actions: Notify Facility Management. Notify Security Team if the damage is intentional, ongoing, or linked to a suspect. Preserve footage and document the affected area. Restrict access if the damage creates a safety hazard.

Incident Type: FIRE
Visual Indicators: visible flames, smoke accumulation, flashing or flickering bright fire-like light, sparks near equipment, sudden spread of smoke through a room, people rapidly evacuating from a localized point.
Interpretation Guidance: If flames or smoke are clearly visible, classify the issue as FIRE even if the exact source is unknown.

Incident Type: THEFT_OR_ROBBERY
Visual Indicators: individual concealing items, forced entry through doors or counters, aggressive confrontation with cashier or staff, weapon visible during interaction, rapid movement toward registers or exits, staff or customers backing away in fear.
Interpretation Guidance: Use THEFT_OR_ROBBERY when the scene suggests active theft, threat, or intimidation connected to taking goods, cash, or access.

Incident Type: VIOLENCE_OR_ASSAULT
Visual Indicators: punching, kicking, shoving, tackling, chasing behavior, one person attacking another, group fighting, crowd panic around a conflict, victim falling to ground after confrontation.
Interpretation Guidance: Use VIOLENCE_OR_ASSAULT when physical aggression is central to the event.

Incident Type: MEDICAL_EMERGENCY
Visual Indicators: sudden collapse, person lying motionless, seizure-like body movement, visible distress or inability to stand, nearby people gathering or signaling for help, staff kneeling beside an individual.
Interpretation Guidance: Use MEDICAL_EMERGENCY when a health crisis appears more likely than violence or intoxication alone.

Incident Type: SUSPICIOUS_BEHAVIOR
Visual Indicators: loitering for unusual duration, repeated pacing or scanning, trying multiple doors, hiding face or unusual concealment, waiting near restricted area without clear purpose, repeated approach and retreat behavior.
Interpretation Guidance: Use SUSPICIOUS_BEHAVIOR when the scene suggests possible preparation, surveillance, or testing behavior without a confirmed active crime.

Incident Type: UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS
Visual Indicators: entering marked restricted area, tailgating behind authorized personnel, opening secure door without permission, bypassing checkpoint or barrier, climbing over gate or fence, remaining in controlled area without authorization.
Interpretation Guidance: Use UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS when the main issue is improper entry into a controlled or restricted space.

Incident Type: FIRE
Playbook: Confirm whether flames or smoke are visible in the feed. Mark incident severity as HIGH. Contact Fire Department immediately. Contact Facility Management. Contact Security Team for evacuation support. Preserve footage and provide location details if known.

Incident Type: THEFT_OR_ROBBERY
Playbook: Confirm whether active theft, threat, or weapon presence is visible. Notify Security Team immediately. Notify Local Police Department if threat is active or weapon is visible. Preserve footage. Track suspect path across cameras if possible. Mark severity as HIGH if weapon or direct threat is present, otherwise MEDIUM.

Incident Type: VIOLENCE_OR_ASSAULT
Playbook: Confirm active aggression or physical conflict. Mark severity as HIGH. Notify Security Team immediately. Notify Local Police Department if conflict is ongoing. Notify Medical Response Team if injuries are visible or likely. Preserve footage and track participants if possible.

Incident Type: MEDICAL_EMERGENCY
Playbook: Confirm collapse, unresponsiveness, or severe visible distress. Mark severity as HIGH. Notify Medical Response Team immediately. Notify Emergency Services if condition appears severe. Notify Security Team to assist access and crowd control. Preserve footage and note location details if known.

Incident Type: SUSPICIOUS_BEHAVIOR
Playbook: Confirm unusual loitering, pacing, door testing, or surveillance-like behavior. Mark severity as LOW or MEDIUM depending on persistence and location. Notify Security Monitoring Team for human review. Escalate to Security Team if restricted areas or repeated attempts are involved. Preserve footage for review.

Incident Type: UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS
Playbook: Confirm entry into restricted area or bypass of access control. Mark severity as MEDIUM unless critical area, weapon, or sabotage risk is present. Notify Security Team immediately. Notify Facility Management if access systems may need inspection. Preserve footage and track the individual if possible.

All model outputs should follow this structure:
incident_type: <FIRE | THEFT_OR_ROBBERY | VIOLENCE_OR_ASSAULT | MEDICAL_EMERGENCY | SUSPICIOUS_BEHAVIOR | PROPERTY_DAMAGE | UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS>
severity: <LOW | MEDIUM | HIGH>
description: <brief operational description>
primary_department: <single department name>
secondary_departments: <comma-separated list or NONE>
recommended_actions: <ordered list of actions>
Rules: Always assign exactly one primary department. Use HIGH severity for fire, active violence, active robbery, or severe medical collapse. Keep description short and operational. Prefer departments that can act immediately.

If a model output is later marked INCORRECT: save the original event summary, the original predicted incident type, the corrected incident type, the corrected primary and secondary departments, and a short note describing why the first prediction was wrong. Retrieval Rule: When new incidents are processed, similar past correction examples may be retrieved and used as few-shot guidance.
